Definition: Upstream smORF located in the 5' UTR of a canonical protein-coding transcript
Upstream smORF present only in a subset of transcript isoforms, leading to ambiguous classification
Upstream smORF that overlaps the initiation codon of a canonical protein-coding sequence
Upstream smORF that ambiguously overlaps the initiation codon of a canonical protein-coding sequence; ambiguity arises from transcript isoforms that may lack the canonical start codon
Downstream smORF located in the 3' UTR of a canonical protein-coding transcript
Downstream overlapping smORF that extends into or overlaps the canonical coding sequence from the 3' region
Downstream ambiguously overlapping smORF; overlap with the canonical coding sequence depends on transcript isoform structure
smORF encoded entirely within the canonical protein-coding sequence region in an alternative reading frame
smORF encoded within an annotated long noncoding RNA
smORF encoded by an alternative reading frame, often arising from alternative splicing and/or supported by entries in the UniProt TrEMBL database scientific conclusions."
